articles.title: Second Venezuelan Doctor Detained in South Texas by Immigration Agents
articles.summary: An E.R. doctor was detained Saturday, just days after a family physician had been detained. Both were traveling when immigration agents took them into custody.

articles.title: What Now? Vance Leaves Iran Talks Without a Deal.
articles.summary: The lack of a breakthrough after 21 hours of negotiations leaves the Trump administration facing several unpalatable options.

articles.title: Trump Was Watching a U.F.C. Fight in Miami While Iran Talks Collapsed
articles.summary: On his way to Florida, President Trump said it did not matter to him if a deal with Iran was reached or not: “We win, regardless,” he said.

articles.title: Judges Fired After Blocking Deportations of Pro-Palestinian Students
articles.summary: The immigration judges’ abrupt dismissals marked the latest efforts by the Trump administration to reshape the country’s immigration courts.

articles.title: White House ballroom construction can continue for now, appeals court says
articles.summary: The order comes as the Trump administration challenges a lower court ruling that the estimated $300-million project requires congressional approval.

articles.title: Push to Expel Swalwell Could Touch Off Chain Reaction of House Removal Votes
articles.summary: The House could move within days to consider expelling Representative Eric Swalwell, Democrat of California, in light of sexual assault allegations against him. There could be more to come for both pa

articles.title: Manhattan DA Opens Investigation Into Eric Swalwell After Sexual Assault Allegations
articles.summary: The Manhattan district attorney’s office is examining a claim that Representative Eric Swalwell, a candidate for governor in California, assaulted a woman in New York City in 2024.

articles.title: Trump touts newly released plans for D.C. triumphal arch
articles.summary: The proposed 250-feet-tall, white-and-gilded monument would stand on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., by the Potomac River.

articles.title: Pakistan hosts historic face-to-face talks between the US and Iran
articles.summary: In Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, officials from the US and Iran met to discuss a possible end to the war that's engulfed much of the Middle East.

articles.title: At a concert in Budapest, anti-Orbán sentiments take center stage ahead of election
articles.summary: At a concert in Budapest, musicians and concertgoers express criticism of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's leadership.

articles.title: How Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's hometown became a symbol of excesses
articles.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has long been accused of corruption. Sightseers now flock to his hometown as groups aim to raise awareness of what they say are the leader's excesses.

articles.title: In Hungary, upcoming elections could bring an end to Orban's 16-year rule
articles.summary: In Hungary, voters head to the polls Sunday. At stake: the future for populist Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, Europe's longest-serving leader - and an ally of Presidents Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.

articles.title: What's it like to cover the Ukraine war as the world's attention is focused on Iran?
articles.summary: In this week's Reporter's Notebook conversation, we take a closer look at what it's like to cover the war in Ukraine, and how the war in Iran is changing that.

articles.title: USPS Is Running Out of Money. Here’s How It Could Affect Your Mail.
articles.summary: Despite numerous attempts to reform the Postal Service, the agency’s business model has not changed significantly since 1970. Officials are proposing to decrease service and increase prices.

articles.title: Postal Service, Under Pressure, Now Faces Trump’s Mail Ballot Order
articles.summary: President Trump’s executive order, which has been challenged as unconstitutional, would limit the Postal Service to sending only the ballots of voters deemed eligible.

articles.title: Pope Leo says 'delusion of omnipotence' is fueling U.S.-Israeli war in Iran
articles.summary: In the first weeks of the war, the Chicago-born Leo was initially reluctant to publicly condemn the violence and limited his comments to muted appeals for peace and dialogue. But Leo stepped up his cr

articles.title: Artemis II splashdown captures nationwide attention
articles.summary: Fans across the country tuned in to see the Artemis II crew make their splashy return to Earth.

articles.title: Opinion: A well-deserved statue for a hero rat
articles.summary: Cambodia is recognizing the life-saving contributions of a rat named Magawa with a statue. The late rat sniffed out landmines for a non-profit group, and in a short career helped find more than 100.

articles.title: India cracks down on satirists for turning its prime minister into a punch line
articles.summary: India's satirists are turning Prime Minister Narendra Modi into a punch line — and the government is hitting back.

articles.title: Wives and children of foreign ISIS fighters stranded in Syria with no way home
articles.summary: NPR visits the last detention camp for ISIS wives and children in an increasingly precarious northeastern Syria.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
